what_it_does: Windows desktop.ini file that maps friendly, localized display names to the actual filenames of PDFs in the folder so Windows Explorer can show them.
observations: Each line maps a display name to a real filename using the Windows format Name=@Filename,0
Names include pitch decks, visual assets, landing mockups, and virtual-tour product sheets
Located in source/dump, not in application source code
standouts: Indexes a set of marketing/pitch and virtual-tour PDFs
Is Windows shell metadata rather than design or runtime code
concerns: Localized display names may obscure the real filenames in the directory
File is metadata and may have been committed accidentally in a source/design dump folder
notes: A Windows Explorer localized-name index for PDF pitch and virtual-tour assets in the dump folder.
questions: Are the referenced PDFs still current deliverables?
Why is a Windows metadata file stored under source/dump?
Are the localized names maintained manually or generated by Windows?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does what desktop.ini [LocalizedFileNames] is meant to do: provide friendly Explorer labels for files.
intention_signals: Catalog and present marketing and design PDFs with human-readable names inside a Windows folder.
contribution: Provides localized display labels for PDF assets in a Windows directory, which may aid browsing but does not contribute build or runtime behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A Windows desktop.ini file in source/dump containing [LocalizedFileNames] mappings for PDF pitch decks, visual assets, landing mockups, and virtual-tour product documents.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to supply friendly Windows Explorer display names for marketing and design PDFs stored in the dump directory.
